_note: CLOB V2 order signing vector, from py-clob-client-v2 tests/order_utils/test_exchange_order_builder_v2.py (RECON-M5-V2). The reference is POLY_1271 (signatureType 3) on AMOY (chainId 80002); its full EXPECTED_POLY_1271_SIGNATURE uses the Solady EIP-7739 TypedDataSign layout: innerSig(65) ‖ appDomainSeparator(32) ‖ contentsHash(32) ‖ contentsType ‖ len. So the leading 65 bytes are the NESTED inner ECDSA sig (not reproducible from the plain order hash); the externally-meaningful checks are the embedded appDomainSeparator (bytes [65:97)) and contentsHash (bytes [97:129)), recorded below. Reproducing those two pins our V2 domain + order-struct hashing exactly. Production differs only in value substitution: chainId 137, signatureType 1 (POLY_PROXY), verifyingContract per neg_risk — same hashing/signing scheme (validated end-to-end by the V1 py-clob-client sigType-1 vectors).
_note_poly1271: FULL EXPECTED_POLY_1271_SIGNATURE from py-clob-client-v2 tests/order_utils/test_exchange_order_builder_v2.py (Solady ERC-7739 TypedDataSign wrap, sigType 3). Layout: innerSig(65) ‖ appDomainSeparator(32) ‖ contentsHash(32) ‖ contentsType(186 ascii) ‖ uint16_be(186)=00ba. For this vector maker=signer=deposit_wallet=0x1111…1111, chainId 80002, neg_risk false. Reproduced byte-for-byte by sign_order_1271.
